name: Sherilyn's Aussie Sausage Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 Pkg Jimmy Dean SAGE sausage, bulk
recipeInstructions:
1. Use one pastry at a time, keeping the other one refrigerated until ready to use.
2. Roll one pastry as thin as possible lengthwise.
3. Mix about 3/4-1 cup fresh breadcrumbs with the sausage to help absorb some of the grease when cooking (makes for better pastry).
4. Place half the sausage mixture on the pastry, about 1 1/2 inches from the edge, lengthwise; roll.
5. Repeat with second pastry and remaining sausage.
6. Glaze by brushing with beaten egg.
7. Using white cotton thread, slip underneath the roll; bring up sides of the thread and criss-cross your hands so that the thread cuts the roll at 2-inch intervals.
8. Place on a baking sheet.
9. Bake at 375°F until golden brown. Not timed - sausage is to be completely cooked. Start checking after 10 minutes. If not cooked, turn them over and continue cooking and watching.
10. Be careful, there is a lot of excess grease left on the baking sheet.
11. Serve with plenty of ketchup for breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
This is one of those recipes that came to me in adulthood. I remember being 16 years old and eating sausage rolls while walking down one of the old "city" streets in Dungog, New South Wales, Australia. The city was built in the 1800s and has a population of a handful. It still looks like time stood still there. The taste of sausage rolls and the memory of Dungog and my precious Nana have never left me. This is an awfully good recipe from "memory." And you know, I even impress my mother with this one!
